_meta.appointmentsPageTerminology: User-facing copy uses Tasks/משימות; the key namespace appointmentsPage is frozen for internal compatibility. The vt_appointments table, /appointments route, and appointmentsPage.* key paths are NOT renamed (Phase 6 §17 forbidden). _meta.* keys are non-rendering metadata (Phase 6 §5 invariant 13) — included in parity, never resolved by user-facing helpers, filtered out of the runtime accessor by stripInternalKeys.

step1.title: Scan your first item
step1.description: Tap the blue scan button at the bottom of the screen to read a QR code. The app instantly loads the status and history.
step1.tip: The scan button is always visible at the center of the bottom bar — you can scan from any screen.

step2.title: Track equipment
step2.description: Open an item and tap 'Check out' to mark it as in your use. Tap 'Return' when done so the team knows where the equipment is.
step2.tip: Items you've checked out appear in the 'Mine' tab in the bottom navigation.

step3.title: Report issues instantly
step3.description: Found damaged equipment? Tap 'Report issue' on the item detail page. The team gets an immediate alert.
step3.tip: You can also report issues from the menu icon in the top right corner.

outcomeKpiDashboard.title: Outcome KPI & ROI
outcomeKpiDashboard.metaDescription: Hospital leadership view: time to triage, handoff integrity, and revenue recovery vs the 14-day pre-activation baseline.
outcomeKpiDashboard.subtitle: Compare the trailing post-activation period to the 14 days before go-live. Requires an activation timestamp in server configuration.
outcomeKpiDashboard.loadError: Could not load outcome KPI data.
outcomeKpiDashboard.noActivationBody: KPI comparisons will be available once an administrator configures the go-live date.
outcomeKpiDashboard.noActivationAdminHint: Set configuration key outcome_kpi_activation_at:<clinic-id> in server settings to an ISO-8601 timestamp value.
outcomeKpiDashboard.kpiTriageDesc: Median minutes from intake to first assignment (P50), same methodology as ER impact metrics.
outcomeKpiDashboard.kpiHandoffDesc: Share of structured clinical handoffs acknowledged directly by the incoming assignee (excludes forced overrides).
outcomeKpiDashboard.kpiRevenueRecoveryDesc: Capture score: 100 minus consumable billing leakage (dispensed vs billed quantities). Higher means fewer missed charges.
outcomeKpiDashboard.methodologyNote: Deltas are oriented so positive percentages indicate improvement (faster triage, higher integrity, stronger revenue capture).

landingPage.seoTitle: VetTrack — Veterinary Equipment QR Tracking System
landingPage.seoDescription: VetTrack — veterinary equipment QR tracking for vet hospitals and ER clinics. Real-time status, offline sync, alerts, and shift handoffs from any phone.
landingPage.seoKeywords: veterinary equipment tracking software, vet hospital QR tracking system, ER veterinary workflow tool, animal hospital equipment management, veterinary management software
landingPage.ogTitle: VetTrack — Veterinary Equipment QR Tracking System
landingPage.ogDescription: Mobile-first QR equipment tracking for veterinary hospitals and ER clinics. Real-time status, offline support, issue alerts, and shift handoffs — all from any device.
landingPage.twitterTitle: VetTrack — Veterinary Equipment QR Tracking System
landingPage.twitterDescription: Mobile-first QR equipment tracking for veterinary hospitals and ER clinics.
landingPage.heroBadge: Built for veterinary ER, ICU & surgery floors
landingPage.heroBody: The mobile operations layer for high-acuity hospitals—QR and NFC in seconds, real-time triage, offline-first sync, and shift-ready handoffs. Like enterprise CMMS, tuned for the pace of a code blue, not a factory line.
landingPage.heroNoCard: No card required to explore the app
landingPage.previewSyncNote: Syncs when connectivity returns — nothing lost in the red zone
landingPage.stat1Label: to log an issue or checkout
landingPage.bentoSubtitle: Scan-first workflows your techs can run between patients—alerts with teeth, a floor map that matches reality, and reporting that holds up in reviews.
landingPage.bento1Body: QR and NFC in the flow of care—no hunting for the right form. Check out, report, and route issues before you leave the bedside.
landingPage.bento2Body: Dead zones in radiology, surgery, and holding? Queue work locally, merge cleanly when you reconnect—UpKeep-style reliability, clinic-sized.
landingPage.bento3Body: Triage that respects urgency. Acknowledge ownership so nothing bounces.
landingPage.bento4Body: See where equipment lives and moves—not a spreadsheet of serial numbers.
landingPage.bento5Body: One-click PDF trails for compliance—maintenance, sterilization, and exceptions.
landingPage.bento6Body: Container-aware inventory sessions for consumables and kits—closer to Sortly precision than a shared cart in the break room.
landingPage.bento7Body: Technicians, leads, and admins see the right surface area—decision support without turning the EMR into the equipment system of record.
landingPage.howSubtitle: The same three beats as a modern CMMS go-live—compressed for a team that can’t pause the floor.
landingPage.how1Body: Print QR from admin, attach to kits and devices. One scan ties physical gear to a living record.
landingPage.how2Body: Staff log checkouts, issues, and room moves from pocket devices—no training binders required.
landingPage.how3Body: Dashboards and PDF exports close the loop for managers and review—alerts first, paper never.
landingPage.quoteBody: We stopped playing telephone about ventilators and monitors. The floor self-organizes when everyone can see the same source of truth.
landingPage.finalBody: Your team is already in motion. VetTrack meets them there—PWA, offline sync, and alerts that respect how critical care actually runs.
landingPage.footerTagline: Clinical equipment operations for modern veterinary hospitals.

helpPage.scanQrDescription: Tap the blue scan button at the bottom to scan equipment. Instantly loads status and history.
helpPage.checkoutDescription: Open an item -> tap 'Check out'. Your name appears on the item so the team knows who has it.
helpPage.returnDescription: Open the item you took -> tap 'Return'. Removes your name and marks it as available.
helpPage.reportIssueDescription: Found damaged equipment? Open item -> tap 'Issue'. The team receives an immediate alert.
helpPage.okDescription: Equipment scanned and ready to use.
helpPage.sterilizedDescription: Item has been sterilized. Will alert when sterilization window expires.
helpPage.maintenanceDescription: Marked for maintenance. Check with admin before use.
helpPage.issueDescription: A fault has been reported. Item should not be used until cleared.
helpPage.inactiveDescription: Item has been removed from service temporarily or permanently.
helpPage.healthRingDescription: The colored ring on each room card shows the percentage of items verified in the last 24 hours. Green >= 80%, Orange >= 40%, Red < 40%.
helpPage.syncedDescription: All items in this room have been verified in the last 24 hours.
helpPage.staleDescription: Room has not been checked in over 24 hours. Tap the room to verify items.
helpPage.auditRequiredDescription: An item in this room was flagged. A full audit is required before the room can be cleared.
helpPage.nfcDescription: Tap the NFC sticker on the room door to open the verification screen. Confirm to mark all items as verified in one tap.
helpPage.pendingDescription: Action saved locally and waiting to be sent to the server. Happens when offline. Will sync automatically when connected.
helpPage.failedDescription: Action could not sync after multiple attempts. Tap the cloud icon to view and retry.
helpPage.offlineDescription: No internet connection. The app continues to work — all changes are queued and will sync automatically.
helpPage.activeAlertsDescription: The red badge on the bell icon shows how many items need attention. Tap to view and acknowledge.
helpPage.pushAlertsDescription: Enable push notifications in Settings -> Push Notifications to receive real-time alerts even when the app is in the background.
helpPage.title: Quick Guide
helpPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know to use VetTrack

roomRadarPage.nfcVerifyAllBody: Tapping this NFC tag marks all equipment in this room as verified. Confirm only if all items are present and accounted for.

roomsListPage.healthRingHelp: Each room card shows a Health Ring: a coloured circle showing what % of items were verified in the last 24 hours. Green ≥ 80%, Amber ≥ 40%, Red < 40%. Stale = not audited in 24+ hours.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.metaDescription: How VetTrack collects, uses, and protects personal data for veterinary hospital staff.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body:
VetTrack provides operational software for veterinary hospitals — equipment tracking, tasks, inventory, and emergency coordination. This policy explains what personal data we process when you use the VetTrack web app, progressive web app (PWA), or native mobile apps, and how we protect it.

VetTrack is intended for hospital staff authorized by your clinic. It is not directed at children.

controller.title: Who is responsible for your data
controller.body:
Your veterinary hospital (clinic) is the primary controller of operational and clinical workflow data created in VetTrack. VetTrack Ltd operates the platform and processes data on the clinic's behalf as a service provider.

For privacy questions about your account or to exercise your rights, contact privacy@vettrack.uk. For product support, contact support@vettrack.uk.

collect.title: Information we collect
collect.body:
Account and identity: name, email address, phone number (when used for sign-in), profile image (if provided), role, clinic membership, and language preference. Authentication is provided by Clerk; Sign in with Apple is available on iOS.

Operational activity: equipment check-outs and returns, scan events, room verification, tasks, inventory actions, Code Blue session participation, shift context, audit log entries tied to your user ID, and support tickets you submit.

Device and technical data: device push notification tokens (if you enable notifications), app version, bounded operational telemetry counters (no free-form labels), error and performance diagnostics when error reporting is enabled, and offline sync queue metadata stored locally on your device.

Device permissions: camera (issue photos and QR scanning), NFC (equipment and room tags), filesystem (attachments), and notifications — only when you grant permission on your device.

use.title: How we use information
use.body:
We use personal data to authenticate you, enforce clinic-scoped access controls, operate equipment and hospital workflows, deliver notifications you opt into, maintain audit trails required for hospital operations, improve reliability and security, and comply with law.

We do not sell personal data. We do not use hospital operational data for unrelated advertising.

processors.title: Service providers
processors.body:
We use trusted subprocessors to run VetTrack, including hosting and database providers, Clerk (authentication), Apple (Sign in with Apple token lifecycle), error monitoring when enabled, and email or notification delivery infrastructure. Each processor is bound by contract to protect data and process it only for providing the service.

Your clinic may configure integrations with external practice-management systems; data shared through those integrations is governed by your clinic's agreements with those vendors.

retention.title: Retention
retention.body:
We retain account and operational records for as long as your clinic uses VetTrack and as needed for legitimate hospital operations, security, and legal obligations. Audit logs may be retained according to your clinic's policy.

When you delete your account, we erase or anonymize personal identifiers as described in our account deletion flow. Some aggregated or de-identified records may remain where they no longer identify you.

security.title: Security
security.body:
VetTrack uses industry-standard safeguards including encrypted transport (HTTPS), role-based access control, clinic-scoped data isolation, encrypted storage for sensitive integration credentials when configured, and monitoring for abuse.

No system is perfectly secure. Report suspected incidents to privacy@vettrack.uk.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body:
Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or export personal data, and to object to certain processing. Clinics in Israel and the United Kingdom may have additional local requirements.

Contact privacy@vettrack.uk to submit a request. We may need to verify your identity and coordinate with your clinic administrator where the clinic is the data controller.

deletion.title: Account deletion
deletion.body:
You can delete your account in the app: Settings → Danger zone → Delete account. Deletion permanently removes or anonymizes your personal data, revokes Sign in with Apple tokens when linked, and signs you out everywhere. Demo or review accounts may be protected from self-deletion.

Deleting your VetTrack account does not automatically cancel an App Store subscription — manage billing in your Apple ID settings.

children.title: Children
children.body: VetTrack is a professional tool for authorized hospital staff. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 13. If you believe a child has created an account, contact privacy@vettrack.uk.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this policy when our practices or legal requirements change. We will post the revised version at this URL and update the "Last updated" date. Material changes may also be communicated through the app or by email where appropriate.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body:
Privacy: privacy@vettrack.uk
Support: support@vettrack.uk
Website: https://vettrack.uk

termsPage.title: Terms of Use
termsPage.metaDescription: Terms governing use of the VetTrack veterinary hospital operations platform.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body:
These Terms of Use ("Terms") govern access to and use of VetTrack — the web app, progressive web app (PWA), and native mobile applications — operated by VetTrack Ltd ("we", "us").

By creating an account or using VetTrack, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

acceptance.title: Agreement
acceptance.body:
Your clinic authorizes staff access to VetTrack. You represent that you are an authorized employee, contractor, or student of a clinic that has contracted for the service, and that you use VetTrack only for legitimate hospital operations.

We may update these Terms. Continued use after the "Last updated" date constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

eligibility.title: Eligibility
eligibility.body:
VetTrack is a professional tool for adults authorized by a participating veterinary hospital. You must be at least 18 years old (or the age of majority in your jurisdiction) to use the service.

Accounts are provisioned or approved by your clinic administrator. We may suspend access for users who are no longer authorized by their clinic.

account.title: Your account
account.body:
You are responsible for safeguarding your sign-in credentials and for all activity under your account. Notify your clinic administrator and support@vettrack.uk immediately if you suspect unauthorized access.

You may delete your own account in Settings → Danger zone → Delete account, subject to clinic policy and applicable law. See our Privacy Policy for how personal data is handled.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable use
acceptableUse.body:
You agree to use VetTrack only for lawful hospital operations and in accordance with your clinic's policies. You must not:

• Access data outside your authorized clinic scope
• Attempt to probe, scan, or compromise the service or other users' accounts
• Upload malicious code or interfere with service availability
• Misrepresent equipment status, audit records, or emergency session data
• Reverse engineer the service except where permitted by law

clinicalDisclaimer.title: Clinical and operational disclaimer
clinicalDisclaimer.body:
VetTrack is an operations platform — not a medical device, electronic medical record, or diagnostic system. Equipment status, inventory counts, and emergency coordination features support hospital workflows but do not replace professional clinical judgment.

Your clinic remains responsible for patient care decisions, regulatory compliance, and verification of operational data.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual property
intellectualProperty.body:
VetTrack, its software, branding, and documentation are owned by VetTrack Ltd or its licensors. We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the service for your clinic's internal operations during your authorized access period.

Operational data created by your clinic in VetTrack belongs to your clinic, subject to our Privacy Policy and data processing terms.

availability.title: Service availability
availability.body:
We aim for high availability but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. Planned maintenance, network outages, device offline mode, and third-party service failures may affect the service.

Offline features queue changes locally and sync when connectivity returns. Emergency mutations require online execution — see in-app guidance for Code Blue workflows.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body:
To the maximum extent permitted by law, VetTrack Ltd is not liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from use of the service, including loss of data, revenue, or clinical outcomes.

Our total liability for any claim relating to the service is limited to the fees paid by your clinic for VetTrack in the twelve months before the claim, or £100 if no fees apply, whichever is greater.

Nothing in these Terms excludes liability that cannot be excluded under applicable law.

termination.title: Suspension and termination
termination.body:
We may suspend or terminate access for violation of these Terms, security risk, or at your clinic's request. Your clinic may deactivate users at any time.

Upon termination, your right to use VetTrack ends. Provisions that by nature should survive (including liability limits, intellectual property, and governing law) remain in effect.

governingLaw.title: Governing law
governingLaw.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales, without regard to conflict-of-law principles. Courts in England shall have exclusive jurisdiction, except where mandatory consumer or employment protections in your jurisdiction provide otherwise.

changes.title: Changes
changes.body: We may revise these Terms when our service or legal requirements change. The current version is always available at this URL with an updated "Last updated" date.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body:
Legal and terms questions: support@vettrack.uk
Privacy: privacy@vettrack.uk
Website: https://vettrack.uk

supportPage.title: Support
supportPage.metaDescription: Get help with VetTrack — account access, technical issues, and App Store support.

overview.title: How we can help
overview.body:
VetTrack support assists authorized hospital staff and clinic administrators with account access, technical problems, and questions about using the platform.

For in-app feature guidance while signed in, open Help from the menu. This page is for account, access, and product support that does not require signing in.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.body:
Email: support@vettrack.uk

Include your clinic name, the email on your VetTrack account, device type (web, iOS, Android), and a short description of the issue. Screenshots help us resolve problems faster.

We aim to respond within two business days. Urgent hospital operational issues should also be escalated through your clinic's internal IT or admin contact.

accountAccess.title: Account and access
accountAccess.body:
New staff accounts are typically created or approved by your clinic administrator. If you cannot sign in:

• Confirm you are using the email or phone number registered with your clinic
• Try password reset from the sign-in screen (Clerk)
• On iOS, Sign in with Apple and Google are available when enabled by your clinic
• Contact your clinic admin if your account is pending approval or blocked

If your clinic admin cannot resolve the issue, email support@vettrack.uk from your work address.

technicalIssues.title: Technical issues
technicalIssues.body:
Before contacting support, try:

• Refreshing the page or force-closing and reopening the app
• Checking network connectivity (VetTrack works offline for many workflows; emergency actions require online access)
• Updating to the latest app version from the App Store
• Clearing browser cache if the web app shows a blank screen after an update

For persistent sync failures, note the time, action you attempted, and any error message shown in the sync queue (cloud icon in the header).

nativeApp.title: iOS and Android apps
nativeApp.body:
The VetTrack native apps are distributed through the Apple App Store (bundle ID uk.vettrack.app). Billing, subscriptions, and refunds are managed by Apple — we cannot process App Store payments directly.

For App Review or TestFlight access issues, your clinic administrator should provide the credentials listed in App Store Connect review notes.

privacyAndDeletion.title: Privacy and account deletion
privacyAndDeletion.body:
Privacy questions: privacy@vettrack.uk — see our Privacy Policy at https://vettrack.uk/privacy

To delete your account: sign in → Settings → Danger zone → Delete account. Deletion is permanent and removes personal data as described in the Privacy Policy.

Deleting your VetTrack account does not cancel App Store subscriptions — manage those in iOS Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions.

emergencyNote.title: Code Blue and emergencies
emergencyNote.body:
VetTrack coordinates Code Blue sessions and emergency equipment workflows but is not an emergency dispatch or medical triage service.

For life-threatening situations, follow your hospital's emergency protocols first. If Code Blue features fail during an active session, use your clinic's offline backup procedures and contact support@vettrack.uk after the incident with timestamps for investigation.
